transcript.text:
(It's the kind of sight that can't be captured all at once...)
Is this a mukbang challenge?
(An amount fit for a mukbang challenge...) Wow, thank you
The amount...
(Jjolmyeon noodles, flat glass noodles, sujebi, egg, seaweed rolls, yaki mandu, mushroom, sundae intestines, tuna rice ball)
(Loaded with extra toppings...)
(No matter how much I eat...)
(It never seems to shrink...)
(Should we use The Gyeonggi Pass and go to the tteokbokki spot?)
Hello, everyone!
I’m at the Gyeonggi Provincial Office today
Actually, I’ve got some great information today!
It's called The Gyeonggi Pass,
a policy that gives partial transportation refunds
If you're a resident of Gyeonggi Province, anyone who uses it
more than 15 times a month

will get a 30% refund if you're between 19 and 39 years old,
and a 20% refund if you're 40 or older
(Children and teenagers (ages 6-18) get a 100% refund!!) Students, even if they don't use it 15 times,
(A limit of 60,000 won per quarter) can still get up to 240,000 won refunded annually!
Actually, just commuting every day
would easily exceed 15 times,
so it’d be a shame not to get the refund!
I got it today!
Just for today, I’m an honorary Gyeonggi resident!
Not a Gyeonggi resident, but I’ll use it to grab some delicious food
Oh! It's here!
(Today's mode of transportation) Do you see that Ttokbus over there?
It's a bus that you can use like a taxi through an app or phone
So cool
The bus is so cute because it's small
Hello

(Taking the Ttokbus and heading out for tteokbokki)
Isn't this so cool?
A bus that comes when you call...
Isn't it really comfy?
The inside is super comfy,
(Spacious and comfortable bus interior...) with really nice seats, and it's so spacious!
There aren't many seats,
so it's pretty private...!
(Transit card transfer applied) Does it allow transfers?
We need to get off and take a different one
(The fare is the same as a regular bus) Transfers are allowed? It's basically just a regular bus!
If you use it too much,
won’t you have to wait like 30 minutes for the bus?
If more people start using it, they’ll add more buses!
That's good
(Shall we set off on a journey with the charming Ttokbus?)

Thank you
(In transit...) I’m heading to transfer now
Because the light is green
Today’s weather is perfect for taking the bus
(Especially with The Gyeonggi Pass... haha) The Gyeonggi Pass
Hello, for 2
- 2? - Yes
(Today’s destination, quickly reached by bus...!)
Arrived!
It's right in front of us now
Wow, it's literally right in front! "Dongtteoki"!
"Dongtteoki" is like a nostalgic spot for students at Ajou University
Wow! It looks like it's right in front of the school!
The budae and veggies are the most popular here!
But since I’m not a fan of vegetables, I’ll go with budae tteokbokki!
Spicy flavor

They only serve up to 4 servings
(Since it's up to 4 servings, I'll add a lot of extras) I added this much extra
(Jjolmyeon noodles, flat glass noodles, sujebi, egg, seaweed rolls, yaki mandu, mushroom, soondae intestines, tuna rice ball)
After taking the bus,
it really feels like I’ve been on a journey, you know?
(A place where waiting is a must on usual days...!) It’s become so popular lately that you have to wait
I’m lucky I came right after lunch hours,
so no waiting!
(Fried food has arrived!) They serve hot fried food
I’ll try one seaweed roll first, I can't wait any longer, I'm so hungry
Oh... This taste...!
It tastes just like the one I had when I was little!
I just got completely dragged back into nostalgia
What's that?
I ordered it thinking lightly,
"It’s for four people, so it won’t be much,"

but the amount...
(Tteokbokki on a mukbang challenge-level scale...) Whoa, thank you
What? Is this a mukbang challenge?
(Making DIY rice balls while the tteokbokki boils...)
The seasoning and flavors are perfect...
It's delicious
The rice balls taste insanely good
It usually comes with pickled radish, but I asked them to leave it out
(Fresh tteokbokki boiling more as I make rice balls)
I think it's time to eat the noodles now
Bon appétit
Since ramyeon needs to be eaten quickly,
I'll eat it first
I know this taste!
I can have one piece of tteok, right?
I’ll try the tteok right now

(Just looking at it, it’s so fluffy...!) This is totally the definition of fluffy tteok, right?
Should I try the eomuk too?
It’s not that it tastes particularly different,
but the familiar taste is surprisingly strong...
The tteok is just... you know what I mean, right?
A soft, tender tteok that cuts easily
Oh! It got cut...
(Fried mandu gotta be dipped, haha) Dipping fried mandu, classic!
(Jjolmyeon > glass noodles, in that order haha) Jjolmyeon soften faster, so gotta eat it first
The portion is huge
It's no joke
I took the bus today
like I mentioned earlier, with The Gyeonggi Pass,
you actually get fully reimbursed
- (Was there such a thing?) - It’s great that all age groups are eligible
(Debit card / credit card / prepaid transit card) and if you issue the card on mobile and use it for transit

(Can be issued with any card, including mobile pay) you can get the refund
I don’t know why it wasn’t around when I was a student...
Wow, there’s a lot of ham inside
I haven’t eaten any ham until now!
It’s a chewy and delicious sausage
This is it
But apparently, this place has been around for 20 years...!
If you go to Ajou University, it’s such a well-known place that everyone knows it,
and isn’t on-the-spot tteokbokki kind of a university staple?
Actually, the first place I ever had on-the-spot tteokbokki
was near a university too!
Isn’t the bus I took earlier pretty unique?
More users means more buses!
But still, how can they
operate at the same price as regular buses?
It even takes me to the exact stop I want!

It's so convenient
Aren’t you from the token ticket generation?
(A word I've never seen before...) Token ticket?
I've only ever used a transport card
- You don't know tokens then? - Token?
Were there copper coins in your generation?
- There were no copper coins - No copper coins?
That's too much...
(Bus token) Students used to buy paper bus tokens
I know what they are! I remembered!!!
You'd cut it
- and hand one piece at a time - Ah... That's not it...
Oh, I!
Do you know that one?
(The subway ticket) The small subway ticket...
(Childhood memories are flooding back...) Was it back in elementary school?

Wow, that just brought back memories!
(Sundae is here) The portion is huge
I think they sprinkled something like perilla seed powder on top, like furikake
The perilla seed powder on top is so savory,
it really matches well! It’s really delicious!
What powder is this?
Gotta dip it in sauce
Soak the lungs in it too
(Hotteok gift...?) They bought me hotteok, amazing
Wow, looks yummy
It's so thin!
What? Where did they buy this?
It's so good
It’s not flowing honey, but more like a sweet filling inside
Wow! That hotteok place?
(Good eatery vibe)

It's a good eatery
The exterior says it’s a great spot
(Thank you to the student) It was brought by the student working here
(who brought the hotteok) It's so tasty, thank you so much
I should buy more on the way home
Egg!
Wow, finally the long-awaited...
You can’t have on-the-spot tteokbokki without an egg, right?
That makes no sense
Gotta crush it...
Wow, just look at this bursting mandu
It's so soft
The portion is huge
Seems like
they really serve a lot here...
It feels like no matter how much I eat, it just never runs out

I tend to eat slowly, so...
For an average person, it's not slow,
but among mukbang YouTubers, I eat really slowly, so everyone is surprised when they watch
"Is that how slowly you eat?"
Since the videos are usually 10-20 minutes long,
people think I finish everything within 30 minutes...
I’m the type to eat slowly without stopping, really
It takes me a really long time when I do mukbang
It’s taking a while today
No matter how much I eat, it never seems to decrease
(I usually eat like this without stopping, haha)
So refreshing
(Already had 5 Coolpis, haha)
(Now it's ad time, haha) Today, I came to shoot a promotional video for The Gyeonggi Pass,
and it turns out that if you're a Gyeonggi Province resident,
it's not just usable within Gyeonggi... It apparently works for all city buses nationwide,

GTX, Shinbundang Line, and even subways!
(Please support The Gyeonggi Pass with great benefits)
I want to eat sweet stuff
Oh right, hotteok!
(Enjoy hotteok while waiting for the fried rice...)
It's cheese rice
It's been a while since I ate cheese rice
(Scraping it all)
This looks amazing
Just by looking, you can tell it’s gonna be insane
I expected fried rice packed with that signature tteokbokki umami,
but the cheese flavor makes it surprisingly mild and nutty
(After finishing off the rest of the fried rice...)
It was my first time having on-the-spot tteokbokki in a long while
I think it tasted especially delicious, maybe because I ate it
in front of the school

or because it’s been so long since I had it
(Back to The Gyeonggi Pass) And can't forget about The Gyeonggi Pass!
There are so many people using buses
and public transport right now,
and it’d be such a waste if you don’t get the refund
A lot of students have been coming in since earlier,
and... haha,
I just want to let them know!
Have you signed up for The Gyeonggi Pass?
The Gyeonggi Pass...
Are you getting a refund with The Gyeonggi Pass?
Since it's 30% without any limit...!
If you ride twice a day,
a 30% refund on a 90,000 won monthly fare
means you get 30,000 won back!
(How many servings of tteokbokki can that buy...?!) It's enough to enjoy a tasty serving of tteokbokki!

If you're curious about how to get the refund,
just visit the K-Pass website to check
and make sure you get it
Once again,
I enjoyed today's meal!
